query: Intel Foundry 18A volume manufacturing yield rate Q4 2026 external customer tapeout status

{"query":"Intel Foundry 18A volume manufacturing yield rate Q4 2026 external customer tapeout status","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1912,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.electronicsweekly.com/foundry/intel-foundry-the-last-chance-2026-05/","title":"Intel Foundry: The Last Chance | Electronics Weekly","content":"CEO Lip-Bu Tan admitted that “the company invested too much, too fast, without sufficient demand,” and Intel’s SEC filing included risk language to the effect that the company has yet to secure meaningful external foundry customers at scale on any of its nodes.
Intel’s 18A PDK 1.0 was released in July 2024, and the company has reported more than 100 tapeouts, but by most industry assessments, it remains far from reaching the level of MHC validation that TSMC has achieved.
On the PDK side, Intel announced the release of 18A PDK 1.0 in July 2024, and tapeouts are ongoing.
Rapidus in Japan is targeting 2nm but has no production track record, with volume manufacturing expected no earlier than 2027.
The report indicated that Nvidia is exploring manufacturing the I/O die for its next-generation GPU architecture, codenamed Feynman (expected around 2028), on Intel’s 18A or 14A process.
### Intel CEO looking beyond 18A and 14A to 10A and 7A"},{"url":"https://gulfsqas.com/expert-time/Intel-Foundry-Takes-Next-Step-18AP-Process-Enters-Risk-Production-Phase-32-11064","title":"Intel Foundry Takes Next Step: 18A-P Process Enters Risk Production Phase - ROA Comparison","content":"Intel Foundry has announced that its Intel 18A-P process technology has moved into the risk production phase, signaling progress in the company’s advanced semiconductor manufacturing roadmap.
Second, this milestone aligns with Intel’s stated goal to offer leading-edge manufacturing services to external customers by 2025-2026.
However, risk production is only an intermediate step."},{"url":"https://intellionaire.substack.com/p/the-intellionaire-ep-24-unofficial","title":"The Intellionaire Ep. 24 - Unofficial Intel Foundry Direct Connect 2026","content":"So, without an official Foundry Direct Connect this year, I thought I would write up my own unofficial summary of Intel Foundry in 2026.
A future variant with further performance and power efficiency gains, 14A-P, should enter production closer towards 2030.
Yields we are told continue to improve each month, with mature/optimal yields expected in 2027.
Last year, Apple sold around 26 million Macs.
Assuming a die size of ~170mm2, and an overall yield of 70%, this would give about 250 good dies per 18A-P wafer.
Considering that Fab 52 + 62 combined are expected to be producing up to 40,000 wafers per month, one could infer that Fab 52 alone, when fully tooled, will be doing 20,000 wafers per month.
Intel 16 went into HVM in Q4 2025."},{"url":"https://semiwiki.com/forum/threads/morgan-stanley-reports-intel-18a-yield-at-50.25207/","title":"Morgan Stanley reports Intel 18A yield at 50% | SemiWiki","content":"Technology analyst Jukan pointed out on social media that, according to a report issued by Morgan Stanley, Intel's 18A process (compared to TSMC's 2nm) has a yield rate of only 50%, and the company is currently working hard to improve its yield level.
As I mentioned before, to my surprise, the yield did increase 7% per month over the last year (1.07x mor3 DPW each month).
i think it's measured relatively like x%x1.07%x1.07% so roughly yield at present x (rate of improvement ) raised to power number of months."},{"url":"https://newsroom.intel.com/intel-foundry/intel-foundry-details-process-milestones-future-innovation-at-vlsi-symposium","title":"Intel Foundry Details Process Milestones and Future Innovation at VLSI Symposium - Intel Newsroom","content":"Intel 18 A-P now in risk production with higher performance, enhanced thermal characteristics and design rule compatibility with Intel 18A
It shared that Intel 18A‑P, the first performance enhancement in the Intel 18A family, has entered risk production, meeting the timeline first shared with customers and partners last year.
20-40% improved thermal resistance through both materials and design innovations."}]}

{"query":"Intel Foundry 18A volume manufacturing yield rate Q4 2026 external customer tapeout status","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.techpowerup.com/350487/intel-solves-18a-yield-issues-production-reaches-30-000-wafers-per-month","title":"Intel Solves 18A Yield Issues, Production Reaches 30,000 Wafers Per Month | TechPowerUp","content":"According to the sell-side equity research firm BlueFin Research Partners, Intel has fixed these yield issues, making the high-volume production of its latest node sustainable from both a manufacturing yield and economic standpoint. For a mature node, this usually means defect rates of D0=0.1 or D0=0.2, and it is likely that Intel has now achieved the lower end of that spectrum with the node advancements and months of 18A high-volume production. This capacity is currently sufficient for Intel's internal production, such as the "Panther Lake" processors, but additional capacity will be needed for other internal products. For the remaining nodes, Intel has started 18A-P risk production at the D1X site in Oregon, with plans to transfer the 18A-P node to Fab 62 for long-term high-volume manufacturing. For external customers, Intel's offerings will focus mainly on 18A-P, 18A-PT, and the upcoming 14A node, which will begin risk production in 2028, with high-volume production slated for 2029.","score":0.8306082,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/frederick-chen-7648bb7_intel-at-barclays-2025-roadmap-manufacturing-activity-7409437569744027650-fqtK","title":"Intel Predicts 18A Yields to Reach Industry Standards by 2026 | Frederick Chen posted on the topic | LinkedIn","content":"[Sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ffrederick-chen-7648bb7_intel-at-barclays-2025-roadmap-manufacturing-activity-7409437569744027650-fqtK&fromSignIn=true&trk=public_post_nav-header-signin)[Join now](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ffrederick-chen-7648bb7_intel-at-barclays-2025-roadmap-manufacturing-activity-7409437569744027650-fqtK&trk=public_post_nav-header-join)[![Image 1](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/frederick-chen-7648bb7_intel-at-barclays-2025-roadmap-manufacturing-activity-7409437569744027650-fqtK)](https://www.linkedin.com/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ffrederick-chen-7648bb7_intel-at-barclays-2025-roadmap-manufacturing-activity-7409437569744027650-fqtK&fromSignIn=true&trk=public_post_nav-header-signin). *   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ffrederick-chen-7648bb7_intel-at-barclays-2025-roadmap-manufacturing-activity-7409437569744027650-fqtK&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting). The Truth About 18A","content":"Intel’s Last Chance? The Truth About 18A
Chips Weekly by Diana
4360 subscribers
168 likes
8130 views
21 Apr 2026
Everyone is calling Intel 18A a comeback story.
But if you look closely — this is not a comeback. It is a survival story.
Intel 18A introduces two major architectural changes at once: RibbonFET gate-all-around transistors and PowerVia backside power delivery. No other chipmaker is attempting both simultaneously. That makes it the most ambitious — and most risky — node transition in the industry right now.
In this video I break down what Intel 18A actually means. Not just as a new technology, but as a turning point for Intel, for the foundry business, and for the global semiconductor supply chain.
⏱️ CHAPTERS
00:00 — Why this is a survival story, not a comeback
00:47 — What RibbonFET and PowerVia actually change
01:50 — Why Intel is taking unusual risk doing both at once
02:39 — Progress is real — but timing is tight
03:31 — Yield: the real battlefield
04:22 — Intel Foundry: the $100 billion bet
05:02 — Customer interest vs. actual commitment
05:43 — The financial pressure is enormous
06:20 — If it works, this changes the industry
06:51 — Why 18A is a geopolitical project
07:23 — The real question
🔔 Subscribe for weekly semiconductor and tech geopolitics analysis.

Before a fabless company can manufacture chips at a given foundry, it needs a PDK, or Process Design Kit. A PDK packages a foundry’s process information into a form that design engineers can actually use. In plain terms, MHC asks: “Does the silicon behave the way the simulation predicted?” How accurately a PDK’s SPICE models reflect the actual behavior of fabricated silicon is one of the most critical measures of a foundry’s technical competence. Intel’s 18A PDK 1.0 was released in July 2024, and the company has reported more than 100 tapeouts, but by most industry assessments, it remains far from reaching the level of MHC validation that TSMC has achieved.","score":0.45914948,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.23,"request_id":"9ad845cd-1e21-466f-be22-c0e0c7ae917e"}
